commit.message:
fix: GUI service X11 auth — add XAUTHORITY + xhost for display access

systemctl start agrikd-gui and desktop shortcut failed silently because:
1. XAUTHORITY env var was missing — X11 server rejected connections
2. xhost didn't grant access to the agrikd service user

Now setup_jetson.sh:
- Auto-detects XAUTHORITY path (gdm, home dir variants)
- Runs xhost +SI:localuser:agrikd for X11 access
- Sets QT_QPA_PLATFORM=xcb for PyQt5
- Adds /run/user to ReadWritePaths for X11 socket access

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: auto-resolve CWD to install root for engine path resolution

Both gui_app.py and main.py now auto-detect the install root on startup:
- Host layout: /opt/agrikd/app/main.py → CWD = /opt/agrikd/
- Docker layout: /app/main.py → CWD = /app/

This ensures relative paths in config.json (models/*, config/*, data/*)
resolve correctly regardless of where the script is launched from.

Previously, running 'python3 /opt/agrikd/app/gui_app.py' from any
directory other than /opt/agrikd/ would fail to find engine files.

commit.message:
fix(#195): switch to PendingIntent OAuth delivery for Samsung compatibility (#198)

* fix(#195): switch to PendingIntent OAuth delivery for Samsung compatibility

Replace ActivityResultLauncher-based OAuth flow with AppAuth's
performAuthorizationRequest() + PendingIntent delivery.

Samsung's aggressive memory management on S23 Ultra (Android 16) kills
AppAuth's AuthorizationManagementActivity while the Chrome Custom Tab is
open, causing the ActivityResultLauncher callback to never fire and leaving
the user stuck on the sign-in screen.

Changes:
- HuggingFaceAuthRepository: replace buildAuthIntent() with startAuthFlow()
  and add deliverAuthResponse(intent) + keep handleAuthResponse(intent)
- HuggingFaceAuthManager: implement startAuthFlow() using
  performAuthorizationRequest() with PendingIntents pointing to MainActivity;
  implement deliverAuthResponse() dispatching token exchange on IO
- AndroidManifest.xml: add android:launchMode="singleTop" to MainActivity
  so onNewIntent() is called on the existing instance instead of re-creating
- MainActivity: inject HuggingFaceAuthRepository; override onNewIntent() to
  detect AppAuth response extras and call deliverAuthResponse()
- OnboardingViewModel/Screen: replace buildAuthIntent()+handleAuthResponse()
  with startAuth(); remove ActivityResultLauncher wiring
- SettingsViewModel/Screen: same replacement as Onboarding

The isAuthenticated StateFlow is updated by deliverAuthResponse() on success,
so the Onboarding and Settings screens react automatically via their existing
Flow collection — no additional wiring needed.

Closes #195

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#195): authResult SharedFlow for UI feedback + Mutex for sign-out race

- Add authResult: SharedFlow<Result<Unit>> to HuggingFaceAuthRepository interface
- Implement _authResult MutableSharedFlow in HuggingFaceAuthManager; emit from deliverAuthResponse()
- Replace ad-hoc CoroutineScope() calls with class-level managerScope (SupervisorJob + IO)
- Add authMutex (Mutex) to serialise handleAuthResponse state-write and signOut
- Restructure handleAuthResponse: token exchange in suspendCancellableCoroutine stage 1,
  persist + state-update under authMutex.withLock in stage 2
- Collect authRepository.authResult in OnboardingViewModel.init → emit AuthSuccess/AuthError events
- Collect authRepository.authResult in SettingsViewModel.init → emit saveSuccess/saveError
- Fix pre-existing OnboardingScreen.kt: uiState.downloadState → uiState.gemmaDownloadState

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#195): move sign-out log inside mutex lock

commit.message:
feat(pro): add sweep engine, analyze command, and profile generation

Phase 3-4 of Ozone Pro diagnostic suite:

sweep.rs — Smart parameter sweep engine:
- Binary search on gpu_layers to find max that fits VRAM budget
- VRAM pruning via planner::estimate_vram_mb() skips OOM configs
- Early stopping when context level exhausts VRAM budget
- Dominated config detection skips redundant benchmarks
- OOM retry with reduced layers
- Pareto frontier tracking with dominance pruning
- Results stored to SQLite automatically

analyze.rs — Benchmark analysis and auto-profiling:
- show_benchmarks: per-model table or all-models summary
- show_pareto: Pareto frontier with profile labels (speed/balanced/context)
- generate_profiles: auto-generates named presets from frontier
- show_profiles: displays stored profiles per-model or globally

main.rs — New subcommands:
- ozone sweep <model> [--max-context N] [--quick]
- ozone analyze [model] [--all] [--generate] [--profiles]

* fix(#196): show retry button when one model errors during parallel download

In the isDownloading branch of the download when block, add a conditional
Retry button that surfaces when uiState.anyError is true. This handles the
mixed state where Gemma-4 enters Error while FunctionGemma is still
Downloading — the ModelDownloadRow already shows the per-model error, but
there was previously no way to trigger a retry until FunctionGemma also
settled.

startDownload(GEMMA_4_E2B) is safe to call here because WorkManager's KEEP
policy leaves an already-downloading or downloaded model untouched, so only
the errored model's work chain is restarted.

commit.message:
test(init): extract make_temp_dir helper to cover cleanup-guard True branch

The seven integration tests in `init.rs` each contained an inline
cleanup guard:

    if tmp.exists() { let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&tmp); }

Because the temporary directory never exists at the start of a fresh CI
run, the True branch (remove existing dir) was never taken.  This left
init.rs at 85.42% branch coverage (7 missed branches out of 48).

The fix follows the established pattern already used in
`workspace_init/mod.rs`:

1. Extract a `make_temp_dir(name)` helper that encapsulates the
   cleanup-before-recreate logic in one place.
2. Add a `make_temp_dir_cleans_up_existing_directory` test that
   pre-creates the directory, then calls `make_temp_dir`, verifying
   that the old tree is removed.  This covers the True branch of the
   helper's cleanup guard.
3. Refactor all seven affected tests to call `make_temp_dir` instead
   of repeating the inline pattern.

Result: init.rs branch coverage 85.42% → 100% (48 → 36 branch points,
0 missed).  Workspace total: 94.41% → 94.71%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
User/yashasg/beatmap improvement (#19)

* fix: derive rhythm positions from song_time, not accumulated dt

Applied the key insight from fizzd's LD48 rhythm game postmortem:
'Every object that needs to be in sync should do so using only the
song position, and NOT anything else.'

scroll_system: rhythm obstacles (with BeatInfo) now compute position
as SPAWN_Y + (song_time - spawn_time) * scroll_speed instead of
accumulating pos.y += vel.dy * dt each frame. This eliminates
floating-point drift that causes obstacles to slowly desync from the
beat grid over time. Non-rhythm entities (particles, popups, freeplay
obstacles) still use dt-based movement.

shape_window_system: window_timer is now derived from
song_time - window_start instead of accumulated dt. Phase transitions
increment window_start by the exact phase duration (not the current
song_time), following the 'never update your reference point
arbitrarily — only increment by set amounts' rule.

Tests updated to advance song_time instead of relying on dt passthrough.

All 383 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use RenderTexture letterbox in Emscripten path to prevent squish

The Emscripten main loop was rendering directly to the screen canvas
(BeginDrawing → render_system → EndDrawing) while the native path
rendered to a 720×1280 RenderTexture then blitted it letterboxed.
This caused the game to stretch/squish to whatever size the browser
canvas was.

Now both paths use the same RenderTexture → DrawTexturePro letterbox
pipeline. The LoopState struct carries the RenderTexture handle so
the Emscripten callback can access it.

Also simplified CSS: removed media queries in favor of a single rule
(max-width:100vw; max-height:100vh; object-fit:contain) that works
for both mobile and desktop since the letterboxing is handled in C++.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: rewrite level_designer with music-driven obstacle assignment

Rewrote level_designer.py based on research from 5argon's notecharting
guide, DDRKirby's Rhythm Quest devlog, and Liang et al.'s PCG paper.

Architecture changed from combo-rotation to 3-phase pipeline:
  Phase 1 (SELECT): density from song structure + flux threshold.
    Repeated section types reuse the same relative beat pattern
    so players experience 'second time = mastery'.
  Phase 2 (ASSIGN): onset passes determine obstacle type directly.
    kick-only → high_bar, hihat-only → low_bar, snare → lane_block,
    multi-pass/melody → shape_gate. Variety injection forces non-shape
    obstacles after consecutive runs, preventing monotony.
  Phase 3 (CLEAN): safety net removes violations (unchanged rules).
    Added gap monotony cleaner: no single gap value > 40%.

Results vs old generator:
  Gap monotony: 20-35% (was 41-47%)
  Hard variety: 73% shape_gate + 20% lane + 6% bars (was 60/32/4)
  Pattern reuse: verse 1 and verse 2 share same beat pattern
  Section-aware: bridge=sparse, drop=dense+consistent, intro=minimal

Regenerated 1_stomper_beatmap.json with new generator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add --difficulty CLI param (easy/medium/hard)

Usage:
  ./build/shapeshifter --difficulty easy
  ./build/shapeshifter --difficulty hard --test-player pro
  ./run.sh --difficulty easy

Defaults to 'medium' when not specified. The difficulty parameter
is passed to load_beat_map() which selects the matching difficulty
tier from the beatmap JSON.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: disable jump/slide — game focuses on shape + lane only

Removed jump (swipe up / W) and slide (swipe down / S) from active
gameplay. The game was too complex with 3 simultaneous mechanics
(shape matching + lane dodging + vertical movement).

Changes:
- player_action_system: removed jump/slide gesture handling
- level_designer: removed low_bar/high_bar from all difficulty tiers
  and section roles
- Regenerated 1_stomper_beatmap.json (shape_gate + lane_block only)
- Updated tests to verify jump/slide are disabled

Engine code for VerticalState/VMode remains intact (dead code) so
the feature can be re-enabled later if desired.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: flow-based shape picker replaces frequency-based assignment

Shape selection now driven by playability flow, not onset pass names.
Based on 5argon/DDRKirby research: 'accuracy to the song is less
important than how fun it feels to play.'

New rules (all in Phase 2 generation, no overlap with Phase 3 cleanup):
1. Section palette: intro/bridge = 1 shape (center lane only),
   verse = 2 shapes (circle+square), pre-chorus/drop = all 3.
   This creates a natural difficulty ramp as shapes are introduced.
2. Short gaps (≤2 beats): repeat same shape (groove/L1-kick principle).
   Player stays locked in rhythm without disruption.
3. Long gaps (3+ beats): switch to different shape from palette.
   Player has time to prepare; variety feels earned.
4. Anti-triple: built into picker, never 3 of same in a row.

Removed dead code: PASS_TO_SHAPE dict, dominant_pass() function.

Regenerated 1_stomper_beatmap.json. Intro is now square-only,
verses use 2 shapes, drops use all 3.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: increase obstacle density and tile patterns across long sections

The beatmap felt too empty — hard mode had 0.27 obstacles/beat with
gaps up to 22 beats (9 seconds of nothing). Two causes:

1. Double-filtering: flux threshold removed 25% of events, then
   section density removed another 20-50%. Raised section densities
   (verse 0.50→0.65, drop 0.80→0.90) and difficulty scales
   (easy 0.50→0.55, medium 0.75→0.80). Lowered flux thresholds
   (hard 25→15 percentile).

2. Pattern stretching: when a 5.7s pre-chorus pattern was reused
   for a 29.7s section, it stretched across 5x the length leaving
   huge gaps. Now tiles the pattern to fill longer sections (e.g.
   29.7s section gets 5 repetitions of the 5.7s pattern).

Results:
  easy:   92→121 obs, 0.7 presses/sec, max gap 18 (intro)
  medium: 100→134 obs, 0.7 presses/sec, max gap 12
  hard:   123→153 obs, 0.8 presses/sec, max gap 13

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: increase hard mode density to ~1 press/sec

Hard: scale 1.00→1.20, flux threshold p15→p5.
Now 171 obstacles at 0.9 presses/sec (was 153 at 0.8/sec).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: lower flux thresholds + skip CI on content/tools changes in PRs

Flux thresholds lowered: easy 70→50, medium 40→25. More musical
events survive filtering, giving denser levels:
  easy:   121→115 obs (slightly fewer due to event selection)
  medium: 134→150 obs (0.33 presses/sec)
  hard:   171 obs unchanged

CI: added paths-ignore to pull_request triggers in all 4 workflows.
Changes to content/, tools/, design-docs/, *.md, *.log no longer
trigger builds on PRs. Push to main still builds everything.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: preserve window shortening and late-spawn clamp under song-time timing

- collision_system: adjust window_start backward instead of advancing
  window_timer when scale < 1.0; shape_window_system re-derives
  window_timer from song_time - window_start every frame so the old
  mutation was silently discarded on the next tick.
- beat_scheduler_system: when late-spawn clamping fires, store an
  adjusted effective_spawn_time in BeatInfo so scroll_system
  reproduces the clamped start_y via its song-time formula.
- Update two existing window_scaling tests to assert window_start
  movement instead of window_timer advancement.
- Add four new tests: BAD/Perfect timing collision, clamped spawn
  time in BeatInfo.

commit.message:
Initial commit: ProguardProtect plugin with 10 R8 crash type detections

- Custom Gradle plugin (lib.proguardprotect) that performs post-build analysis
  of APK/AAB artifacts using mapping.txt + DEX inspection
- Detects 8 crash types: reflection class/method/field, Gson serialization,
  enum stripping, callback interface removal, devirtualization, ServiceLoader
- Multi-variant support: registers tasks for all productFlavors × buildTypes
- Dynamic APK/AAB discovery (no hardcoded filenames)
- AAB support via dexdump-based DEX extraction from bundle zip
- Dark-theme HTML report with source code references and fix suggestions
- Demo app with 4 product flavors (free, paid, demo, internal) × release build
- All 10 issues detected from APK and AAB, 0 false positives with fixes applied
- KDoc on all plugin classes, comprehensive README
